title: Task Packet
description: A generic task packet exchanged between an orchestrator and a worker adapter. Carries slice identification, task type, payload, and runtime context. Designed to be tool-agnostic so any adapter (Cursor, Cline, OpenHands, etc.) can process it.

task_type.description: The kind of work this task represents.

slice_ref.description: Reference to the slice this task implements.

slice_id.description: Slice identifier from the dependency tree, e.g. ADAPTER-SPEC-1.

slice_number.description: Numeric rank of the slice in the backlog.

dependency_tree_path.description: Path to the dependency tree JSON used to select this slice.

slice_detail_dir.description: Path to the slice detail directory, if one exists.

orchestrator_ref.description: Identifies which orchestrator run dispatched this task.

run_id.description: Unique identifier for this orchestrator run.

orchestrator_type.description: The orchestrator that dispatched the task.

dispatched_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the task was dispatched.

adapter_type.description: The target adapter that should process this task. Determines routing.

payload.description: The actual task content. Structure depends on task_type. Adapters use this to determine what work to perform.

instructions.description: Natural-language or structured instructions for the worker.

working_directory.description: Repository root or working directory for this task.

branch_prefix.description: Branch prefix convention for this task. Prefer agent/ for new work; cursor/ remains valid during migration.

target_branch.description: The base branch the worker should target with changes.

context.description: Non-secret runtime context and credential references.

credential_refs.description: Names of credentials the worker may need. Actual values are resolved by the adapter or environment, never embedded here.

github_slug.description: Short GitHub slug for the target repo.

model_hint.description: Optional model preference for AI-powered adapters. Not a hard constraint.

tags.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata for routing, filtering, or observability.

ttl_seconds.description: Optional time-to-live in seconds. If the task is not picked up within this window, it may be considered stale.

priority.description: Task priority. Lower numbers = higher priority.
